## Freelance Service Agreement – Brand Refresh 2025

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between TechStartup Inc., a California corporation, with its principal place of business at [Client Address], acting through its Marketing Director, Sarah Johnson (the "Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, a California professional entity, with its principal place of business at [Freelancer Address], acting through its Senior Designer, Alex Chen (the "Freelancer"). Individually, each may be referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, the Client desires to engage the Freelancer to perform creative services in connection with the Brand Refresh 2025 project, specifically a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document;

WHEREAS, the Freelancer represents that they possess the necessary qualifications, skills, and experience to provide such services;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants set forth herein, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (13 total)

### Definitions

*Type: standard*

1.1 "Services" means all creative design, consultation, and deliverables described in Section 2 of this Agreement.
1.2 "Project" refers to the Brand Refresh 2025 project as described in the recitals above.
1.3 "Deliverables" means the specific items detailed in Section 2.2 below.
1.4 "Confidential Information" means any nonpublic information or materials disclosed by either Party in connection with this Agreement.

### Scope of Services and Deliverables

*Type: standard*

2.1 The Freelancer shall provide the following services: complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and design of a brand guidelines document.
2.2 The Freelancer shall deliver the following Deliverables according to the following schedule:
   (a) Logo concepts (3 options), due January 30, 2025
   (b) Selected logo refinement, due February 15, 2025
   (c) Brand guidelines document, due March 1, 2025
   (d) Asset package delivery, due March 15, 2025

### Timeline and Milestones

*Type: standard*

3.1 The Project shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude upon delivery of the final asset package on or before March 15, 2025, unless terminated earlier in accordance with Section 11 of this Agreement.
3.2 All Deliverables shall be completed and provided to the Client in a timely manner according to the schedule set forth in Section 2.2.
3.3 Time is of the essence in the performance of this Agreement.

### Payment Terms

*Type: standard*

4.1 The total fee for Services provided under this Agreement shall be Eight Thousand Five Hundred U.S. Dollars ($8,500).
4.2 The Client shall pay the Freelancer a deposit of $2,550 upon execution of this Agreement. The deposit is non-refundable except in the case of uncured breach by Freelancer under Section 11.
4.3 Payments for Deliverables shall be made according to the following milestone schedule:
   (a) $2,000 upon delivery of logo concepts (3 options), due January 30, 2025
   (b) $1,500 upon delivery of selected logo refinement, due February 15, 2025
   (c) $2,500 upon delivery of the brand guidelines document, due March 1, 2025
   (d) $2,500 upon delivery of the asset package, due March 15, 2025
4.4 All invoices are due within fourteen (14) days of receipt. Late payments shall accrue interest at the rate of 1.0% per month, or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is less.

### Revisions

*Type: standard*

5.1 The Client is entitled to up to three (3) rounds of revisions for each major Deliverable as outlined in Section 2.2. Any additional revision requests beyond three (3) rounds per Deliverable shall be billed at the Freelancer's standard hourly rate of $150 per hour, subject to the Client's prior written approval.

### Intellectual Property Assignment

*Type: standard*

6.1 Upon full payment of all fees, the Freelancer hereby assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest, including all copyrights, in and to the final versions of the Deliverables (excluding any pre-existing proprietary materials and tools of the Freelancer).
6.2 Prior to full payment, all intellectual property rights remain vested in the Freelancer.
6.3 Freelancer retains the right to display Deliverables in portfolios and for self-promotional purposes, provided that such use does not disclose Client Confidential Information or infringe on Client’s trademarks.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

7.1 Each Party agrees to maintain as confidential all Confidential Information disclosed in connection with this Agreement, and not to use or disclose such information except as required to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
7.2 This Section shall survive termination of the Agreement.

### Independent Contractor Status; AB5 Compliance

*Type: standard*

8.1 The Parties acknowledge and agree that the Freelancer shall perform all Services as an independent contractor. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create any partnership, joint venture, employer-employee, or agency relationship between the Parties.
8.2 The Parties agree to comply with the requirements of the California Labor Code AB5 and the ABC test as set out in California law. The Freelancer has the autonomy to control and direct the manner and means by which the Services are performed.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

9.1 EXCEPT FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OR GROSS NEGLIGENCE, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT.
9.2 THE FREELANCER'S TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ANY AND ALL CLAIMS ARISING UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT PAID BY CLIENT UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Warranties and Disclaimer

*Type: standard*

10.1 Freelancer warrants that the Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and, to Freelancer's knowledge, shall not infringe or misappropriate any third party rights.
10.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED HEREIN, ALL SERVICES AND DELIVERABLES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS," WITHOUT ANY ADDITIONAL WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.

### Termination and Cancellation

*Type: standard*

11.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon fourteen (14) days’ prior written notice to the other Party.
11.2 Upon termination, Client shall pay Freelancer for all Services rendered and costs actually incurred up to the effective date of termination. Any non-refundable deposit shall be credited against such amounts due.
11.3 If either Party breaches any material provision and fails to cure such breach within ten (10) business days after receipt of written notice, the non-breaching Party may terminate this Agreement immediately.
11.4 Upon termination, Freelancer shall deliver to Client all work completed to date, subject to payment obligations.

### Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

*Type: standard*

12.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.
12.2 Any disputes arising under or related to this Agreement shall be submitted to binding arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association, with the venue exclusively in the county where the Client maintains its principal place of business in California.
12.3 Each Party shall bear its own legal fees and costs, except as otherwise ordered by the arbitrator. Judgment on any arbitration award may be entered by any court of competent jurisdiction.

### General Provisions

*Type: standard*

13.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, whether oral or written, relating to its subject matter.
13.2 Amendment. Any amendment or modification to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both Parties.
13.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party.
13.4 Notices. All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered personally, by overnight or certified mail, or by electronic mail with delivery confirmation, to the addresses specified by each Party.
13.5 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held void or unenforceable, such provision will be severed, and the remainder of the Agreement will remain in full force and effect.
13.6 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
13.7 No Waiver. The failure of either Party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other provision.
